cases.prompt:
This sequence is generated by a periodic recurrence over modular arithmetic. For n >= 4: a[n] = (p[n mod 4]*a[n-1] + q[n mod 4]*a[n-2] + t*a[n-3] + r*n + s) mod M. The unknown modulus M is exactly one of 97, 101, or 103. The 8 periodic coefficients p[0..3], q[0..3] are each one of -2, -1, 1, or 2. The global coefficient t is also one of those four values. The coefficient r is a nonzero integer from -4 through 4, and s is an integer from -4 through 4. Remainders are reported in the range 0 through M-1. Indexing begins at n=1. Infer and verify the unique system, then return the next 3 terms.

Sequence: 17, 90, 85, 28, 87, 69, 82, 17, 52, 62, 69, 50, 50, 86, 70, 80, 27

Assign twelve compute jobs to machines W, X, Y, and Z. Each machine must receive exactly three jobs. Minimize total cost while satisfying every interaction. The optimum is unique, but several feasible assignments are close in cost. Return the complete assignment and exact total cost.

Job | W | X | Y | Z
--- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---:
J1 | 22 | 20 | 3 | 21
J2 | 14 | 28 | 5 | 22
J3 | 27 | 18 | 34 | 27
J4 | 18 | 7 | 3 | 11
J5 | 32 | 10 | 27 | 35
J6 | 35 | 4 | 14 | 24
J7 | 13 | 25 | 8 | 9
J8 | 27 | 8 | 27 | 7
J9 | 9 | 14 | 29 | 23
J10 | 19 | 4 | 21 | 4
J11 | 35 | 31 | 12 | 9
J12 | 18 | 13 | 4 | 4

Constraints:
- J2 and J11 must use different machines.
- J10 and J12 must use different machines.
- J1 and J8 must use different machines.
- J4 and J11 must use the same machine.
- J9 may use only X or Y.
- J6 may use only W or Y.
- J1 may not use X.
- J3 may not use Z.
- If J12 uses Z, then J10 may not use X.
- If J7 uses X, then J8 may not use W.
- If J2 uses X, then J11 may not use W.
- If J4 uses Z, then J5 may not use X.
- Exactly one of J8, J4, and J3 must use W.
- Exactly one of J7, J3, and J4 must use X.

Each person is either truthful or a liar. A truthful person's entire nested statement is true, while a liar's entire statement is false. T(X) means X is truthful. AND, OR, NOT, XOR, IFF, and IMPLIES have their standard Boolean meanings; OR is inclusive. EXACTLY(k; ...) is true when exactly k listed expressions are true. Determine the unique type of every person.

- A says: "OR(XOR(T(F), T(Q)), NOT(T(P)))."
- B says: "IMPLIES(XOR(XOR(T(I), T(L)), NOT(T(N))), AND(OR(T(Q), T(H)), AND(T(M), T(A))))."
- C says: "IFF(IMPLIES(XOR(T(Q), T(F)), OR(T(P), T(M))), XOR(EXACTLY(2; T(Q); T(G); T(J)), AND(T(P), T(L))))."
- D says: "OR(OR(T(C), T(G)), XOR(OR(T(F), T(N)), T(J)))."
- E says: "IMPLIES(EXACTLY(2; T(O); T(F); T(A)), EXACTLY(1; T(H); T(Q); T(G)))."
- F says: "AND(IMPLIES(XOR(T(O), T(M)), EXACTLY(2; T(D); T(C); T(N))), XOR(OR(T(J), T(D)), EXACTLY(2; T(A); T(H); T(K))))."
- G says: "OR(OR(T(Q), T(F)), EXACTLY(1; T(O); T(Q); T(J)))."
- H says: "IFF(XOR(T(A), T(E)), XOR(OR(T(B), T(Q)), OR(T(M), T(B))))."
- I says: "AND(XOR(T(D), XOR(T(E), T(H))), AND(AND(T(A), T(N)), AND(T(N), T(Q))))."
- J says: "IFF(IMPLIES(EXACTLY(2; T(G); T(F); T(L)), NOT(T(G))), IFF(XOR(T(H), T(G)), AND(T(F), T(C))))."
- K says: "IMPLIES(OR(NOT(T(P)), EXACTLY(2; T(O); T(D); T(I))), IFF(AND(T(C), T(O)), T(Q)))."
- L says: "OR(IMPLIES(T(D), EXACTLY(1; T(P); T(Q); T(A))), IFF(XOR(T(K), T(M)), AND(T(G), T(Q))))."
- M says: "AND(XOR(T(E), T(A)), IFF(T(C), NOT(T(I))))."
- N says: "XOR(OR(T(I), T(D)), T(Q))."
- O says: "AND(T(E), EXACTLY(1; T(P); T(D); T(Q)))."
- P says: "IFF(AND(T(M), T(N)), XOR(T(I), T(O)))."
- Q says: "IMPLIES(IMPLIES(XOR(T(G), T(P)), EXACTLY(1; T(L); T(G); T(F))), IFF(AND(T(N), T(K)), OR(T(C), T(O))))."

Each person is either truthful or a liar. A truthful person's entire nested statement is true, while a liar's entire statement is false. T(X) means X is truthful. AND, OR, NOT, XOR, IFF, and IMPLIES have their standard Boolean meanings; OR is inclusive. EXACTLY(k; ...) is true when exactly k listed expressions are true. Determine the unique type of every person.

- A says: "OR(OR(NOT(T(N)), OR(T(F), T(K))), XOR(XOR(T(L), T(N)), XOR(T(K), T(I))))."
- B says: "XOR(IMPLIES(EXACTLY(1; T(N); T(F); T(A)), AND(T(L), T(O))), XOR(T(C), T(Q)))."
- C says: "AND(EXACTLY(2; T(G); T(F); T(D)), XOR(T(J), T(G)))."
- D says: "OR(AND(T(N), T(L)), AND(T(I), T(Q)))."
- E says: "AND(IMPLIES(XOR(T(L), T(A)), XOR(T(C), T(P))), IFF(EXACTLY(2; T(A); T(C); T(P)), EXACTLY(2; T(G); T(L); T(B))))."
- F says: "IFF(AND(T(O), T(M)), NOT(T(N)))."
- G says: "IFF(NOT(T(L)), AND(T(D), T(B)))."
- H says: "IMPLIES(IMPLIES(XOR(T(E), T(B)), XOR(T(I), T(D))), XOR(OR(T(B), T(K)), XOR(T(F), T(O))))."
- I says: "AND(OR(NOT(T(O)), T(G)), AND(T(N), T(K)))."
- J says: "IMPLIES(AND(T(K), T(D)), EXACTLY(1; T(F); T(L); T(M)))."
- K says: "XOR(XOR(T(L), OR(T(B), T(M))), IMPLIES(AND(T(M), T(C)), EXACTLY(1; T(F); T(M); T(Q))))."
- L says: "OR(AND(NOT(T(P)), XOR(T(E), T(P))), XOR(XOR(T(F), T(B)), NOT(T(Q))))."
- M says: "IFF(IMPLIES(EXACTLY(2; T(F); T(L); T(P)), XOR(T(P), T(C))), XOR(XOR(T(O), T(J)), NOT(T(K))))."
- N says: "IMPLIES(IFF(XOR(T(I), T(K)), T(L)), OR(NOT(T(A)), AND(T(D), T(H))))."
- O says: "OR(IFF(T(L), OR(T(P), T(J))), IMPLIES(NOT(T(M)), OR(T(D), T(G))))."
- P says: "IFF(NOT(T(G)), AND(T(N), T(O)))."
- Q says: "OR(AND(NOT(T(P)), OR(T(B), T(A))), OR(OR(T(H), T(F)), OR(T(C), T(L))))."

Each person is either truthful or a liar. A truthful person's entire nested statement is true, while a liar's entire statement is false. T(X) means X is truthful. AND, OR, NOT, XOR, IFF, and IMPLIES have their standard Boolean meanings; OR is inclusive. EXACTLY(k; ...) is true when exactly k listed expressions are true. Determine the unique type of every person.

- A says: "XOR(AND(XOR(T(F), T(M)), AND(T(C), T(N))), OR(NOT(T(B)), NOT(T(I))))."
- B says: "IFF(AND(T(D), T(K)), NOT(T(O)))."
- C says: "OR(XOR(EXACTLY(2; T(G); T(H); T(K)), XOR(T(I), T(D))), OR(NOT(T(F)), AND(T(J), T(A))))."
- D says: "IMPLIES(OR(T(F), T(B)), EXACTLY(2; T(N); T(K); T(M)))."
- E says: "XOR(AND(T(I), T(J)), AND(T(I), T(A)))."
- F says: "OR(AND(T(N), T(A)), AND(T(B), T(A)))."
- G says: "IMPLIES(AND(AND(T(H), T(C)), AND(T(D), T(F))), OR(EXACTLY(1; T(P); T(I); T(N)), T(A)))."
- H says: "AND(IMPLIES(EXACTLY(2; T(N); T(F); T(C)), EXACTLY(1; T(L); T(C); T(F))), IFF(AND(T(I), T(C)), OR(T(G), T(F))))."
- I says: "IMPLIES(OR(OR(T(K), T(J)), T(Q)), AND(EXACTLY(2; T(J); T(L); T(Q)), XOR(T(F), T(E))))."
- J says: "OR(AND(T(P), T(A)), OR(T(C), T(E)))."
- K says: "IFF(IFF(T(H), NOT(T(F))), IMPLIES(EXACTLY(1; T(P); T(H); T(N)), XOR(T(I), T(O))))."
- L says: "IMPLIES(XOR(AND(T(I), T(C)), OR(T(P), T(C))), OR(AND(T(P), T(H)), T(H)))."
- M says: "OR(OR(EXACTLY(1; T(C); T(D); T(F)), XOR(T(N), T(A))), IMPLIES(XOR(T(I), T(C)), EXACTLY(1; T(C); T(I); T(E))))."
- N says: "XOR(IMPLIES(AND(T(D), T(P)), OR(T(L), T(H))), IMPLIES(XOR(T(I), T(L)), AND(T(E), T(Q))))."
- O says: "XOR(XOR(XOR(T(C), T(D)), OR(T(L), T(J))), XOR(AND(T(G), T(E)), EXACTLY(1; T(J); T(D); T(A))))."
- P says: "XOR(OR(XOR(T(F), T(H)), OR(T(A), T(Q))), AND(AND(T(C), T(L)), T(M)))."
- Q says: "OR(EXACTLY(2; T(L); T(O); T(F)), OR(T(C), T(H)))."

Each person is either truthful or a liar. A truthful person's entire nested statement is true, while a liar's entire statement is false. T(X) means X is truthful. AND, OR, NOT, XOR, IFF, and IMPLIES have their standard Boolean meanings; OR is inclusive. EXACTLY(k; ...) is true when exactly k listed expressions are true. Determine the unique type of every person.

- A says: "OR(AND(XOR(T(I), T(G)), EXACTLY(2; T(D); T(L); T(C))), IFF(T(G), NOT(T(M))))."
- B says: "IMPLIES(NOT(T(I)), EXACTLY(2; T(I); T(F); T(H)))."
- C says: "OR(T(H), EXACTLY(1; T(F); T(I); T(P)))."
- D says: "OR(AND(NOT(T(E)), XOR(T(J), T(B))), AND(T(G), EXACTLY(1; T(B); T(O); T(C))))."
- E says: "IMPLIES(IFF(XOR(T(H), T(N)), XOR(T(G), T(B))), IFF(AND(T(O), T(I)), T(Q)))."
- F says: "IFF(OR(EXACTLY(2; T(P); T(L); T(O)), EXACTLY(2; T(A); T(H); T(O))), XOR(T(B), EXACTLY(1; T(C); T(L); T(I))))."
- G says: "XOR(IFF(XOR(T(P), T(K)), OR(T(H), T(I))), IMPLIES(NOT(T(L)), T(L)))."
- H says: "IMPLIES(OR(OR(T(P), T(D)), OR(T(O), T(E))), IMPLIES(XOR(T(E), T(M)), NOT(T(P))))."
- I says: "IMPLIES(XOR(NOT(T(O)), NOT(T(G))), OR(T(M), AND(T(H), T(O))))."
- J says: "IFF(AND(T(H), T(I)), EXACTLY(1; T(B); T(I); T(E)))."
- K says: "IMPLIES(OR(T(G), T(N)), XOR(T(E), T(Q)))."
- L says: "XOR(AND(T(F), OR(T(H), T(A))), IFF(OR(T(O), T(H)), XOR(T(K), T(B))))."
- M says: "IMPLIES(AND(T(J), T(B)), EXACTLY(1; T(J); T(F); T(O)))."
- N says: "AND(IFF(T(O), OR(T(A), T(J))), IMPLIES(AND(T(P), T(L)), NOT(T(B))))."
- O says: "IMPLIES(T(F), EXACTLY(2; T(M); T(L); T(B)))."
- P says: "AND(OR(NOT(T(M)), AND(T(C), T(J))), IFF(OR(T(K), T(I)), T(L)))."
- Q says: "IMPLIES(OR(XOR(T(L), T(I)), NOT(T(F))), OR(T(A), NOT(T(O))))."

The unknown modulus M is exactly one of 97, 101, or 103. The 8 periodic coefficients p[0..3], q[0..3] are each one of -2, -1, 1, or 2. The global coefficient t is also one of those four values. The coefficient r is a nonzero integer from -4 through 4, and s is an integer from -4 through 4. Remainders are reported in the range 0 through M-1. Indexing begins at n=1.
